desc:
The air is cool here as you walk upon the road, leading you east or west through
the bushland. As you stop and survey your progress, you notice a dark shadow
pass over the prairie and even the thorny shrubs scattered throughout seem to
shiver at its passing, or is it just a trick of the eye and a play of the wind?
Feeling cold that emanates not so much from the outside as within, you feel an
urge to quicken your journey.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You are standing on the Old East Road as it makes its way east-west through some
gently rolling plains. In the distance to the southeast, the land becomes much
rougher. Rising above the road to the east are some jagged hills. There doesn't
seem to be much living here besides you and the grass covering the plains.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road continues making its way east-west through the rolling plains. You feel
that you are all alone in this wide land. To the east, jagged, broken hills
block the road's easy path, while, to the southeast, the plains become rougher.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road winds its way east and west here, skirting around the broken plains and
hills of the Lone-lands to the east. The land to the north is more hospitable,
but no less empty. You begin to wonder why there aren't any people living in
these lands.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road winds its way west and east here, skirting around the sheer cliffs that
form this side of the hills to the east and around the broken plains to the
south. Coming closer to the hills, you feel a sense of foreboding. Perhaps in
among the hills is the answer to the emptiness of these lands.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road makes its way here east-west, skirting the broken hills of the
Lone-lands to the south. To the north, the rolling plains continue, broken only
by some trees in the distance. The jagged cliffs to the south seem to be
impassable, except for the faint remains of a path leading up into them to the
south.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road winds east and west here, making its way between the jagged, broken
hills to the south and the rolling plains to the north. The plains to the north
and west stretch as far as the eye can see, broken only by an occasional stand
of trees. The hills to the south seem impassable.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Here, the road runs east-west between the rolling plains to the north and the
broken plains of the Lone-lands to the south. To the southwest lie an area of
cliff-like broken hills. This land seems to be truly desolate.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Running east-west, the road threads its way between the rolling plains to the
north and the broken terrain of the Lone-lands to the south. To the southwest,
the land rises up into an area of broken hills. Directly north of you, a thicket
provides some welcome cover.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road runs north and west here, right under the shadow of the southern hills.
The rolling plains to the north do little to warm your soul that has been
chilled here under what seems to be the hateful watch of the hills.
name: A Bend in the Old East Road

desc:
Curving south and east, the road curves around the battered terrain of the
Lone-lands to the south. Southwards rise some broken hills, while southeast, the
terrain is slightly more manageable. To the north lie gently rolling plains.
name: A Bend in the Old East Road

desc:
The road runs east-west here between the rolling plains to the north and the
broken plains to the south. To the southwest, the Lone-lands rise up into a
series of hills.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Still travelling down this road, your surroundings make the Lone Lands seem seem
an appropriate name. The only life you can discern in this desolate realm of
Arda, are rabbits scuttling across the road and in the grass.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You push your way into a stand of gigantically tall grey weeds which stand like
spears, pointing skywards. They rise to heights which dwarf even you and their
stems grow as thick as your wrist. `These are not ordinary weeds', you think to
yourself and you even imagine you hear a low humming noise among them.
name: A Tall Stand of Weeds

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. The
flats become absorbed here by the weeds and brush, retreating towards the west.
name: The Flats

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
Everywhere you turn, all you can see are the gently rolling grasslands, except
to the west where there is a band of evil-looking shrubs. You can't even see the
Old East Road. Off in the distance, to the southeast, you can see rocky hills
rising up above the plains.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The gently waving grass of this rolling land almost makes you feel at peace. A
glance at the rocky hills in the southeast dispels those feelings, filling you
with a sense of dread. The rolling plains continue in all directions with the
Old East Road to the north of you.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You stand amid the waving grass of some gently rolling plains. Everything seems
unnaturally silent. Casting a glance at the rocky hills to the southeast, you
feel a shiver. Perhaps it is something in those hills. To the north, you can see
the Old East Road half-hidden in the tall grass, and, beyond it, a dark stand of
trees in the distance.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Here a rocky spur juts out from the hills to the southeast and stabs northward
at the road. The rocks are barren and cracked, and burned to a pale, dusty grey
by the sun's heat. An ancient track can just be made out, winding along the
flank of the spur and climbing to the south.
name: An Abandoned Path in the Broken Hills

desc:
Normally, a walk through grasslands like this would be quite pleasant. Under the
brooding gaze of the hills to the south, however, make you feel more as if
you're trying to sneak past a watchdog. To the north and west, the grasslands
take you further from the brooding menace to the southeast. South and east of
you is the Old East Road; beyond which, the land rises up into shattered hills.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You stand amid the broken hills of the Lone-lands. The hills drop off into sheer
cliffs in all directions, except to the east. To the east, you can make out the
remains of a path. To the northeast, your eyes are drawn to an ancient ruined
tower on an overlook.
name: Broken Hills

desc:
What little of the path you see winds its ways through the tortured terrain of
the hills, but it generally runs east-west. You are definitely going up into the
hills, even though you can't see much. While looking around, you feel chill in
the pit of your stomach as you glimpse the top of a tower beyond a hill to the
northeast.
name: An Abandoned Path in the Broken Hills

desc:
Although the path has been winding its way through the cliffs that make up these
hills, it makes a definite turn from north to east here. The desolation of this
place is only made worse by the impression that this path has not been used for
ages.
name: An Abandoned Path in the Broken Hills

desc:
You can just make out the path as it winds it way from the road to the north up
into the hills to the south. This path does not appear to have been used for
some time. The only reason you are able to follow it is because there is no
other way to go in this broken terrain.
name: An Abandoned Path in the Broken Hills

desc:
You stand amid the waving grass of some gently rolling plains. Everything seems
unnaturally silent. Casting a glance at the rocky hills to the northeast, you
feel a shiver. Perhaps it is something in those hills. To the south, the land
becomes more rocky and desolate.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The silence of the Lone-lands surrounds you. Broken only by the buzz of insects
and the rustle of the wind, you feel compelled to keep the silence. The rocky
hills to the southeast make you feel afraid to be heard. The gently rolling
plains continue in all directions, broken only by a stand of trees to the north.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The occasional buzz of an insect and rustle of wind in the grass are the only
things that break the monotony of these grasslands as they march off in all
directions. Several miles northward, you can see the Old East Road as it winds
its way through the Lone-lands. In the distance to the east, you can see the
land rise up into some broken hills.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You stand knee-deep in the tough grass that covers these gently rolling plains.
The plains stretch off to the east and to the south where you can see some low
but rough hillocks on the horizon. North of here the land is covered by thick,
thorny vegetation.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
weather you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
Like the waves of the ocean, the grasslands undulate around you. Not a soul is
to be seen. Perhaps why this land is called the Lone-lands. The plains continue
in all directions except to the west, where can be seen a band of dense shrubs.
You can see a stand of stunted trees to the northeast and some rock hills far to
the southeast.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The silence of the Lone-lands surrounds you. Broken only by the buzz of insects
and the rustle of the wind, you feel compelled to keep the silence. The rocky
hills to the southeast make you feel afraid to be heard. The gently rolling
plains continue in all directions, broken only by a stand of trees to the north.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing where the land starts to become rougher, and more desolate. The
land here looks as if some giant hand had reached down and crumpled the earth
like a piece of paper. Your way is blocked to the east by a sharp cliff that is
the start of some broken hills. The land smoothes out to the north and west
where the East Road winds far in the distance. The dry plains continue to the
south.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
You are standing in the little bit of shade provided by the cliff that rises to
the west. Growing up against the rock is a tangle of plant-life. South of you is
a dense thicket that promises to offer more shade than this cliff. To the north
and east, the dry, tortured lands of the Lone-lands continues.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
This old, forsaken path continues up into the broken hills of the Lone-lands
from the west to the north. Beyond some hills to the north, you can see the
battlements of a shattered tower. You have the eerie feeling that you are not
alone in these shattered lands.
name: An Abandoned Path in the Broken Hills

desc:
At last! Some real shade. You have managed to find a hollow in the rock that is
protected by a tangle of vegetation. Not only is it out of the elements, but
there's also a small spring that provides a trickle of water for this natural
shelter. You feel safe enough in here to rest and regain your strength before
going back out and battling with the harsh terrain of the Lone-lands.
name: A Protected Hollow

desc:
The remains of this path lead from the broken hills to the south to a hill
overlooking the lands to the north. Atop that overlook, you see the ruins of a
tower. The hills of the Lone-lands surround you, the silence of which you expect
to be shattered by a scream at any instant.
name: An Abandoned Path in the Broken Hills

desc:
You stand amid the broken hills of the Lone-lands. To the south and east, the
land drops away in sheer cliffs to the broken plains below. To the west, you
think you can make out a path. To the northwest, an ancient tower sits atop an
overlook.
name: Broken Hills

desc:
You stand at the northern most end of the broken hills of the Lone-lands. The
land drops off in sheer cliffs to the north and east. To the north, beyond the
broken plains below, you can make out the Old East Road. To the west, an ancient
tower stands on an overlook.
name: Broken Hills

desc:
You are standing at the top of an overlook at the northern end of the broken
hills that make up this part of the Lone-lands. The hills continue to the south
and east, with the remains of a path leading south. Before you is the towering
bulk of an ancient tower. Glancing down at the road below, you see the shadow of
the tower, a shadow shaped like a cruel orkish dagger. You feel cold, a cold not
from the breeze, but from the Shadow of the tower.
name: Ruined Overlook

desc:
You are standing in the end of a small gully. To the west, the cliff- like side
of a hill rises up. To the north and east, you are faced with a steep,
gravel-strewn slope that is all but impassable. Your only clear way is to the
south. You feel like hurrying onwards, trapped as you are here. You feel a
presence, like a vulture circling, waiting for your death.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
You are standing next to a rock face to the west that stretches north and south.
All around you are reminders of how desolate these lands are. You don't see any
significant animal life. The plant life consists mostly of tough, dry grass, but
you can see a small stand of stunted trees off to the northeast. An impassable
jumble of rocks blocks your path to the north, but the way east and south is
clear. You feel a chill, as if something, something Evil, is watching you.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
You pause a moment to wipe the sweat from your brow and only manage to move
around a pasty mixture of sweat and dust. To the north, a stand of stunted trees
grow, promising some shade within. Eastwards, your path is blocked by a
cliff-like outcropping, but the way south appears clear. The way is clear to the
west, beyond which some evil-looking hills rise.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
You are standing among some trees. They appear to be coniferous, although you
can't quite identify their type. The trees are spaced well enough apart to allow
some smaller plants to grow in the rich mulch provided by the bed of old
needles. The stand continues to the north, while the broken plains continue in
the other directions. You aren't sure, however, if you'll be able to get back up
the gravelly slope to the west.
name: A Stand of Stunted Trees

desc:
You are standing among some trees. They appear to be coniferous, although you
can't quite identify their type. The trees are spaced well enough apart to allow
some smaller plants to grow in the rich mulch provided by the bed of old
needles. The stand continues to the south, while the broken plains continue to
the east and west. To the north, you can see the Old East Road, beyond which,
the land becomes more grassy and gently rolling.
name: A Stand of Stunted Trees

desc:
All around you, the land is strewn with rocks. As if some giant hand had reached
down, tore up a section of Endor and threw it back down. You feel very uneasy
here, like a rabbit that has wandered too far from its hole. Looking at the
rocky hills off to the west simply intensifies this feeling. A rocky outcropping
blocks travel to the east, but you can make your way through this broken land to
the north west and south.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
Even though the water from the basin has long ago departed, this cool shady cave
has preserved a tiny amount in the form of some sticky mud. The unpleasant smell
tells you that it is now a dwelling-place for a creature that might not care
much for intruders.
name: A Muddy Cave

desc:
Surrounded by scrubby trees and wavy grass, you are standing in a shallow basin
that appears to have held water in the distant past. Small rocks and bleached
sticks make up most of the floor of the basin, while the sides are loose earth
and gravel. To the north and west you may enter sickly groves of stunted trees.
To the south, the land rises up an abrupt and difficult rocky slope. It is not
easy to see how high it extends.
name: A Shallow Basin

desc:
This gentle hillside was once covered with small hardy trees able to survive
under these inhospitable conditions. Now, however, all that remain in this
fire-ravaged area are gnarly stumps and soot encrusted sticks.
name: A Charred Slope

desc:
Knee-deep in grass, you pause to look around at your surroundings. Off to the
south, beyond the Old East Road to the south, the land becomes rougher and more
rocky. Southwest, the land becomes actual hills that give you a chill just
looking at them. To the west, the plains continue. To the east, a dense thicket
grows where you can rest in relative safety.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing waist-deep in the tough grass that covers these gently rolling
hills. To the south lies the Old East Road, beyond which, the land rises up into
shattered hills that cast a shadow over your heart as it does the road. Atop the
hills stand a dark shadowy tower. The plains continue to the east, while a stand
of trees grows to the west.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing on the edge of an expanse of rolling plains. The grass comes to
your knees and waves gently in the breeze. The idyllic setting is spoiled,
though, by a malevolent presence from the broken hills to the south beyond the
Old East Road. The road curves by to the west and south. The plains continue to
the west, while a stand of trees grows to the north.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The grass of these rolling plains brushes gently at your knees as you survey
your surroundings. To the north and west of you grows a stand of trees, while
the rolling grasslands continue to the east and south. Further to the south are
some broken hills, the sight of which makes you blood run like ice water.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing among some trees. They appear to be coniferous, although you
can't quite identify their type. The trees are spaced far enough apart to allow
some smaller plants to grow in the bed of old pine needles. The stand continues
to the west and to the north where you can hear the bubbling sound of a spring.
Beyond the trees, to the east and south, the rolling plains continue.
name: A Stand of Stunted Trees

desc:
You are standing among some trees. They appear to be coniferous, although you
can't quite identify their type. The trees are spaced well enough apart to allow
some smaller plants to grow in the rich mulch provided by the bed of old
needles. The stand continues east, while the rolling plains continue in all
other directions.
name: A Stand of Stunted Trees

desc:
Pausing a moment to mop your brow, you survey your surroundings. To the south,
the gently rolling plains continue without much change. To the east, a stunted
stand of trees grows. To the north, a dense thicket promises a safe place to
rest without notice. To the west, the plains change to dry flatlands.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. These
flats stretch away are bordered to the west and south by a band of shrubs,
giving way to gently rolling plains to the north and east.
name: The Flats

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slowly and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
Knee-deep in grass, you pause to look around at your surroundings. Off to the
south, beyond the Old East Road to the south, the land becomes rougher and more
rocky. Southwest, the land becomes actual hills that give you a chill just
looking at them. To the west, the plains continue. To the east, a dense thicket
grows where you can rest in relative safety.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing waist-deep in the tough grass that covers these gently rolling
hills. To the south lies the Old East Road, beyond which, the land rises up into
shattered hills that cast a shadow over your heart as it does the road. Atop the
hills stand a dark shadowy tower. The plains continue to the east, while a stand
of trees grows to the west.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing among some trees. They appear to be coniferous, although you
can't quite identify their type. The trees are spaced well enough apart to allow
some smaller plants to grow in the rich mulch provided by the bed of old
needles. The stand continues to the west, while the rolling plains continue to
the east and south.
name: A Stand of Stunted Trees

desc:
You are standing among some trees. They appear to be coniferous, although you
can't quite identify their type. The trees are spaced far enough apart to allow
some smaller plants to grow in the bed of old pine needles. The stand continues
to the east and to the west where you can hear the bubbling sound of a spring.
Beyond the trees, to the south, the rolling plains continue.
name: A Stand of Stunted Trees

desc:
You are standing in a circle of trees. They appear to be coniferous, although
you can't quite identify their type. A spring flows here, forming a pool among
the trees. Surrounding the spring, are a variety of plants growing in the bed of
pine needles. The stand of trees continues to the south and east.
name: A Circle of Trees

desc:
The tall, waving grass of this gently rolling land looks inviting. It would be
pleasant to lie down for a while, but you think it would be wiser to rest in the
shelter of the thicket to the west of you or in the trees that are south and
east you.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
All around you, the dense vegetation screens you from the worst of the sun and
anyone (or anything) passing by. You feel safe in here, making this an ideal
place to rest. Looking out of the thicket, you can see that the plains continue
to the east, south, and west. To the southeast, you can see a stand of trees.
name: A Dense Thicket

desc:
You are standing in the deep grass of gently rolling plains. You see no signs of
civilisation anywhere. To the west, the grasslands give way to some evil looking
flats. To the east is a dense thicket, while to the south a band of shrubs
promises to make passage difficult.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it.
name: The Flats

desc:
You push your way into a stand of gigantically tall grey weeds which stand like
spears, pointing skywards. They rise to heights which dwarf even you and their
stems grow as thick as your wrist. `These are not ordinary weeds', you think to
yourself and you even imagine you hear a low humming noise among them.
name: A Tall Stand of Weeds

desc:
This shallow ravine leads into the small basin to your west. It seems to have
carried water many ages ago. As silent testimony to the long gone water, trees
that once flourished are now dead and rotten. Some small trees, however, are
still clinging to life, evidenced by a few tiny leaves on their branches. The
draw continues to the east.
name: A Wooded Draw

desc:
The floor of the draw is covered with sand and smooth pebbles. Apparently the
water that once flowed in this channel washed away all of the soil components
leaving these remains behind. Small lizards occasionally appear through cracks
in the riverbed, only to disappear in a blink of an eye.
name: A Wooded Draw

desc:
The water that was once here played a vital role in the growth of the
surrounding trees. Judging from the size of the dead stumps and fallen branches,
the trees must have been huge, full of leaves and life. That was long ago
however, as all that remains is sandy soil and deadwood. Peering through the
deadened trees to the south, you see an empty plateau which seems to end
abruptly a stone's throw away.
name: A Wooded Draw

desc:
This ancient, dry streambed winds further back into the plateau. Sometimes it
broadens to a flat basin, floored by red-brown sand; sometimes it is a barely
passable stairway climbing up rock shelves over which the stream doubtless fell
in merry cascades in ages past. Rock walls, eroded by the wind into fantastic
shapes, rise on either side; clumps of ferns can be seen sprouting from the bare
rock, high up, nourished by a moister layer of the stone.
name: A Wooded Draw

desc:
An ancient dry ravine cuts through the rocky plateau, sloping from its heights
to the south downward toward the west. Here and there are old stone basins at
the foot of waterless falls, filled with rounded pebbles, red, yellow,
blue-grey, worn smooth by vanished cascades. Pools which once were filled with
clear water are now flat sandy expanses, marked by the trails of snakes, by the
marks of little clawed feet, and perhaps by other, subtler marks as well.
name: A Rocky Gully

desc:
The gully continues on its miles-long path which it has cut through the soft
rocks of this plateau. Here its floor is broad and sandy, and clumps of hardy
grass eke out a living from the rainwater. Birds of prey can be glimpsed against
the sky from time to time. Barring a few odd markings in the sand, there is no
sign that anyone has ever lived in these parts.
name: A Rocky Gully

desc:
A narrow gully winds southward out of the highest parts of this plateau. Here it
takes the form of a steep-walled ravine or canyon. The walls are unclimbable,
having been smoothed flat by wind and rain to the height of many men. The floor
of the gully is stony and broken into myriad shelves and stages, down which
water plunged in the distant past. Now only snakes and spiders haunt the sand
and stones, it seems.
name: A Rocky Gully

desc:
Making your way through a sea of straw, you see the grass concede to a squalid
stand of shrivelled trees to your south. Eastward the ground slopes upward to
richer grassland. A short walk north will bring you onto a road running east and
west across the plains.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
Here the plains rise slightly from the Old East Road to the north. The many
miles of plains stretch all around you. To the south is a small draw that looks
as though it once carried water. The bank is too steep to negotiate travel to it
from here, perhaps there is a way around. Across the draw is a cliff that looks
like the rim of a deep canyon.
name: High Plains

desc:
Slackly the plains rise up, letting you look down on the road which lies north
and east of here. To your south, a draw bordered by withered trees ends the
plains' dominion. Gazing beyond the draw, you see a ridge rising up and spanning
east. Turning east, you notice that the land becomes more hilly as it descends
toward the ford across Mitheithel river.
name: High Plains

desc:
Slackly the plains rise up, letting you look down on the road which lies north
and east of here. To your south, a draw bordered by withered trees ends the
plains' dominion. Gazing beyond the draw, you see a ridge rising up and spanning
east. Turning east, you notice that the land becomes more hilly as it descends
toward the ford across Mitheithel river.
name: High Plains

desc:
Slackly the plains rise up, letting you look down on the road which lies north
and east of here. To your south, a draw bordered by withered trees ends the
plains' dominion. Gazing beyond the draw, you see a ridge rising up and spanning
east. Turning east, you notice that the land becomes more hilly as it descends
toward the ford across Mitheithel river.
name: High Plains

desc:
There is no way to tell if this path has been used recently. The presence of
fresh growth would indicate that the trail was old and forgotten. However, the
lack of moisture makes the sprouting of anything almost impossible. The surface
of the ground is rock hard, making it very difficult to track anything.
name: A Dry Path

desc:
There is no way to tell if this path has been used recently. The presence of
fresh growth would indicate that the trail was old and forgotten. However, the
lack of moisture makes the sprouting of anything almost impossible. The surface
of the ground is rock hard, making it very difficult to track anything.
name: A Dry Path

desc:
Making your way through a sea of straw, you see the grass concede to a squalid
stand of shrivelled trees to your south. Eastward the ground slopes upward to
richer grassland. A short walk north will bring you onto a road running east and
west across the plains.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
Here the plains rise slightly from the Old East Road to the north. The many
miles of plains stretch all around you. To the south is a small draw that looks
as though it once carried water. The bank is too steep to negotiate travel to it
from here, perhaps there is a way around. Across the draw is a cliff that looks
like the rim of a deep canyon.
name: High Plains

desc:
You stand on a plain that is somewhat elevated from the road, giving you a good
view of the lay of the surrounding land. The Old East Road, which runs north of
here, sweeps nonchalantly around the bushes, trees and small hills that lie on
these plains. Looking southwest, you can barely make out a lone spire fighting
for attention amongst the sullen hills.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The thick grass continues, seeming never to end as you gaze across the open
plain. Looking north, the road winds its way across the Lone-Lands. To the south
and east, only more plains await. Nothing about this area is openly stimulating,
in fact, quiet rolling plains like this might make for a nice place to take a
nap.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Slackly the plains rise up, letting you look down on the road which lies north
and east of here. To your south, a draw bordered by withered trees ends the
plains' dominion. Gazing beyond the draw, you see a ridge rising up and spanning
east. Turning east, you notice that the land becomes more hilly as it descends
toward the ford across Mitheithel river.
name: High Plains

desc:
Slackly the plains rise up, letting you look down on the road which lies north
and east of here. To your south, a draw bordered by withered trees ends the
plains' dominion. Gazing beyond the draw, you see a ridge rising up and spanning
east. Turning east, you notice that the land becomes more hilly as it descends
toward the ford across Mitheithel river.
name: High Plains

desc:
The reason for the sparse life here is apparent. Food, habitat, and water are
obviously deficient. Any beast living in such a place must be a fierce forager
indeed. These trees bear no fruit and give little cover or shade. It is so dry
here, the slightest spark could set the place ablaze.
name: A Stunted Forest

desc:
The woods of this little forest are barely surviving the heat and drought. Many
have already succumbed to the grim conditions and stand like silent sentries
watching the road. Their leafless branches offer little shade and even less
protection from the elements. The only perfect use of this wood, seems to be
fuel for a fire.
name: A Stunted Forest

desc:
The trunks of this small forest are withered and cracking. Their bark is peeling
off in dry strips, making funny corkscrew shapes as it breaks off and falls to
the ground. The wood that remains is very dry and would catch fire quickly. It
would be wise to keep a firm grip on any open flame here.
name: A Stunted Forest

desc:
This area just off the road is scattered with dried out bushes and stumps.
Tumbleweeds that have rolled into the bushes make an impenetrable wall of
growth. They lodge onto anything that will stop their momentum. However, in the
middle of a storm, all those weeds might work to keep out the wind.
name: A Roadside Thicket

desc:
The bleakness of the land around you makes you appreciate even the most ordinary
things usually taken for granted. Your senses dull from lack of input as the
land around you seems to swallow any sight, smell or noise of any redeeming
quality.
name: Old East Road

desc:
When the sun beats down on this road laid down long ago, every landmark is seen
as if through a film of yellow-grey. Rubble and dirt whirls up from underneath
your feet, indicating just how dry and unsustaining the soil of the surrounding
plains must be.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The worn road is surrounded by plains to the north and south. The grassy plains
continue out of eyesight to your north, unperturbedly growing and expanding, as
they have since the year 1409, when this land was burnt and pillaged by the
armies of the Witch-king on their march to Amon Sul.
name: Old East Road

desc:
There is little to be impressed with on this stretch of road. To the south and
east lies more of the road, while the vast plains continue to surround you. You
estimate that this is about the halfway point on the road between the town of
Bree in the west and the Elven refuge of Rivendell in the east.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Visible in the west is the massive Weathertop. As for the road you are on, its
dust and grit seem to clog the very workings of your body. As you walk between
the vast stretches of plains, you seem very small and insignificant.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You seem to be in the heart of the Lone-Lands now. Crossing the vast plains in
an east-west direction, the Old East Road marks the only break in the tall
grass. On either side of the road, the familiar rolling hills stretch out for
many a farthing. Far to the south you think you spot a canyon, but from this
distance you cannot be sure.
name: Old East Road

desc:
A lifeless forest accompanies the roadside view at this point. Westward you can
see the Old East Road lying in a straight line, while the forest to your
northeast forces the road toward the Last Bridge to bend south.
name: Old East Road

desc:
As you continue down this lonesome road, all you can do is shuffle your feet.
Even the dust you kick up seems more alive than the surrounding land. You strain
to glimpse some sign of life, inwardly knowing it is futile.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road here is dusty and cracked from lack of moisture. With luck this area
might get precipitation enough to offer some relief but this seems doubtful.
Most days the sky remains clear sky and the drown still thirsts, conditions that
often cause travellers to check that their precious water is securely tucked
away before trudging onward. To the south you discern a small path.
name: Old East Road

desc:
This small but relatively deep valley was created by the slopes of adjacent
hills. It is shallow enough that you can climb out easily but deep enough to be
invisible from the road to the South. The east slope is steeper and has a few
loose planks neglectfully left there.
name: A Dip in the Grass

desc:
Crossing this vast stretch of grassy plain is going to take all the resolve you
can muster. Even you may not have enough to cross this sea of grass. It is so
endless that you wonder if, by crossing it, you will end up in the northern
wastes of Arda.
name: Endless Plains

desc:
Following no pattern, heeding no laws, the long grass blows carefree in the
wind. Rolling with the gentle contours of the land, you note little change in
the surroundings. The grass goes on for as far as the eye can see. To venture
into such lands, one must have brought plenty of provisions.
name: Endless Plains

desc:
The horizon seems to contain only more of the green-brown shoots of grass that
you have seen bordering the road for the last few farthings. It goes on and on,
farther than you think you can ride in a day or even a week.
name: Endless Plains

desc:
The rolling plains goes on and on, covered with a dry grass that the smallest
fire would set to fire. The interspersion of brown thickets of growth and rocky
soil, gives it only a tinge of variety. The ground is in several places littered
by small but sharp stones, able to cut the leather of a boot or wound the hooves
of a horse. Southward the slope of a low but rocky hill is covered by such
stones.
name: Endless Plains

desc:
All around you is the same old grass. Even a solitary flower would seem out of
place in these dull brown plains. Broken only occasionally by sparse patches of
rocky soil and scraggly thickets, the monotony is overwhelming. Dropping a torch
here would be most unwise, as the lack of water makes everything very dry.
name: Endless Plains

desc:
A wagon of unknown origin has been left here on the fringe of the forest.
Obviously abandoned long ago, you wonder which cruel fate befell its owners. The
wagon is in shambles, two of the four wheels broken, and the cloth top has all
but disintegrated.
name: A Wrecked Wagon

desc:
You seem to have found the home of some wild beasts. The earth has been carved
out from around the wagon by the wear of the wind, making a perfectly hollow
niche under the floorboards. It seems that whatever lives here is quite
comfortable. You see scraps of meat and fur from rabbits on the ground. There is
a pile of bones in the corner.
name: Under the Wagon

desc:
The smell of smoke is still strong here, but the heat of the flames has dropped
from such painful proportions. There is just enough relief to wipe the sweat
from your brow and clear your eyes. The rest may be brief however, as fire can
spread quickly in these open plains. Perhaps the rocks to your east may provide
more stable shelter.
name: Smoky Plains

desc:
This is an area of the plains saved from the wrath of the fire. Spared only
because the ground is littered with broken chunks of rock, left behind from a
massive upheaval of the earth. Some of the rocks are too big to move, but poking
around some of the little ones may unearth some treasure perhaps.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
Huddled in this dense growth of plants, you feel that this is a safe place to
rest, hidden from any wild animals that might pass by. Peering out of the
thicket, you can see that the plains continue to the east and west, and the Old
East Road lies just to the south.
name: A Dense Thicket

desc:
You stand amid the rolling plains of the Lone-lands. All around you is the same
knee-high grass. The plains continue to the east and west. The Old East Road
makes its way to the south, beyond which the land grows rocky. To the southwest,
in the distance, you can see forbidding cliff-like hills rising above the broken
landscape.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Before you lies farthing after farthing of waist-high wavy grass. As the wind
blows the stalks to and fro, it rolls like waves of the distant sea. It makes a
dry rustling sound as the shoots brush together.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
As you push the planks aside, a draft of cool air wafts out in your face.
Apparently you have wandered into some sort of dwelling. There is little to
notice, just a table, chairs, and a few boxes scattered about. The spartan
existence along with the isolated location makes you think that the inhabitants
are either very poor, or on the run from something.
name: Behind the Planks

desc:
Cleverly concealed from the road, this hollowed out slope is a perfect place to
hide from the nearby travellers. The furnishings suggest that there is constant
activity here. There are water barrels and clothes on the floor, while in a
corner you see various weapons lying about. It occurs to you that this sort of
place would be good for an ambush, or just to hide stolen goods.
name: A Hideout in the Plains

desc:
You are standing on the main level of an ancient ruined tower. Judging by the
style of the construction and furniture remains, this tower was built by the
ancient Numenoreans. There does not seem to have been any sign of a battle, yet
it seems that some tragedy has befallen this place. Ancient, crumbling stairs
lead up and down from here. There is also a door leading out to the south.
name: Inside a Ruined Tower

desc:
You are standing on the stairs leading down to the cellar of the tower. The
sense of decay and abandonment grows stronger the further down you go, seeping
into the very stone you stand on. You're afraid that if you linger here much
longer, the ancient stone will give way, plunging you down into the darkness.
name: Stairs to the Cellar

desc:
There is a sense of death and decay down in this tower's cellar that comes from
more than just the remains of barrels and sacks that once held fresh supplies.
Not a single ray of light reaches this place making it extremely difficult to
see just what is here. The air is stale and the surroundings dismal. Those that
find themselves here want to leave this evil place but might hesitate, afraid to
risk the stairs.
name: Ruined Cellar

desc:
You are on the stairs leading from the main level of the tower to its upper
gallery. All around you, the beautiful stonework...what was that? Did the floor
just shift? Maybe you should get off these stairs before they give way.
name: Stairs to the Gallery

desc:
You are standing in the upper gallery of the tower. Judging by the furnishings
surrounding you, the floor served as the living quarters for the soldiers
stationed here. Stairs lead up and down from here. The evil presence that you
have been feeling seems to be coming from up the stairs.
name: Ruined Upper Gallery

desc:
You are on the stairs leading from the tower's upper gallery to the roof. All
around you, the signs of decay are obvious, including these stairs. The stairs
feel as if they are about to give way at any moment. Travel upwards is impeded
by a closed hatch in the ceiling.
name: Stairs to the Roof

desc:
You are standing on the roof of the ancient tower. Some magical force has
managed to cloak the roof in darkness, even in the full light of day. Oddly
enough, you can still see the surrounding lands. This tower was obviously set
here to watch over the road. Looking around, you see that the only way to leave
the roof is by the hatch set in the floor.
name: Roof of a Ruined Tower

desc:
This is the valley of a small, muddy stream, the banks of which have become so
overgrown with a jungle of thorny bushes and vines that the walls of vegetation
have met overhead to form a ceiling of interlaced canes and creepers. There is
no direct sunlight here at all; when it is noon outside and above, it is
twilight here. The streambed is flat and stony; otherwise thornbushes would be
growing everywhere, and this vacuity would not exist. The water trickles
mournfully from pool to pool.
name: Thorny Cavern

desc:
This is a long, stone-vaulted passage, now sunk hip-deep in sand and peaty mud,
leading under the East Road and carrying a trickle of water southward. The
roof-stones seem sturdy, but trickles of water leak around them here and there,
and pale dribbles of lime have solidified as dirty, fragile stalactites. Strange
footprints can be seen in the mud - almost human, yet smaller, and clawed.
name: A Dark and Muddy Culvert

desc:
The vast expanse of grass lasts well beyond your eyesight. The most significant
deviation in the monotonous scenery comes from patches of rocky soil where the
grass has not been able to penetrate. Common sense tells you to stock up on food
and water before going much further into these parts.
name: Endless Plains

desc:
Whether started by you or someone else, this fire is out of control. You cannot
see farther than in front of your face. Your eyes sting from the billowing smoke
and it is so hot it burns!
name: A Raging Brush Fire

desc:
Whether started by you or someone else, this fire is out of control. You cannot
see farther than in front of your face. Your eyes sting from the billowing smoke
and it is so hot it burns!
name: A Raging Brush Fire
